data.narrative:
Rocket Factory Augsburg is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage bracket, including Sierra Space, Cowboy Space Corporation, Varda Space Industries, Aalyria, and Skyryse.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $80M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $152M. Peer-median benchmarking across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically do not release.

The two applicable methods produce a wide triangulated range of $129M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $826M. Stage-progression yields the lower bound, reflecting early-stage capital efficiency, while peer median anchors toward the upper range. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in private aerospace valuations, where peer outcomes diverge significantly based on technical maturity, customer traction, and regulatory pathway. Revenue disclosure would substantially narrow this band.
